nouns.summary: The phrase presents “Βίβλος” as the main object of the title, indicating that what follows is the name of a book.
“γενέσεως” specifies the subject matter of the book, indicating that the book concerns the generation or genealogy being referenced.
“Ἰησοῦ” identifies the person whose generation/genealogy is being referenced, and he is explicitly described in relation to other figures in the title.
“Χριστοῦ” is used together with “Ἰησοῦ” to denote the full identity/title “Jesus Christ,” as part of the title’s description of the referenced person.
“υἱοῦ” functions to state lineage relationships in the title, indicating that Jesus is “son of” the following named individuals.
“Δαυὶδ” names one of the people identified as the father/lineage source in the title, connected to Jesus through the descriptor “son of.”
“Ἀβραάμ” names another person tied to Jesus by lineage in the title, again expressed through the phrase “son of.”
